risk_name: Site Conditions — Ground Contamination, Archaeology, or Geotechnical Surprises

description.summary: Unexpected ground conditions (contamination, poor geotechnical properties, archaeological finds, unexploded ordnance) discovered during site preparation cause construction delays and cost increases. Brownfield industrial sites carry contamination risk; greenfield sites carry geotechnical uncertainty.
description.detailed: Hydrogen projects at industrial sites face site condition risks: (1) Brownfield sites: soil and groundwater contamination from previous industrial use (hydrocarbons, heavy metals, asbestos). HGHH at former coal plant site required coal ash remediation. (2) Greenfield sites: geotechnical uncertainty (bearing capacity, groundwater, settlement). (3) All sites: archaeological chance finds (common in Europe), unexploded ordnance (WWII-era in Germany, France, Belgium, Netherlands). Contamination remediation can cost €1-10M and add 3-12 months. Archaeological finds can stop work for weeks to months while authorities investigate.
description.root_cause: Incomplete site investigation during feasibility. Brownfield contamination from previous industrial use. Geological/archaeological uncertainty inherent to all construction.
trigger_events: Site investigation during FEED identifies contamination exceeding feasibility study assumptions
Ground investigation reveals bearing capacity below foundation design assumptions
Archaeological find during excavation stops work on affected area

preventive_actions.description: Conduct comprehensive geotechnical and environmental site investigation during FEED — not at construction start. Minimum 3 boreholes for 2 ha site.
Include soil remediation contingency of €2-5M and 6-month schedule float in brownfield project budget

incident_descriptions: Hamburg Green Hydrogen Hub (GA-PR-004): Former Moorburg coal-fired power plant (1,600 MW, operated 2015-2021). Site required: coal plant dismantling (completed 2024 before hydrogen construction started), coal ash pond remediation, existing infrastructure assessment for reuse (380 kV connection, water treatment, buildings). The 2022 announcement → Dec 2025 foundation (3+ years) includes site preparation. The brownfield premium is offset by reused infrastructure value.

sources.title: HGHH (GA-PR-004) — Moorburg coal plant repurposing: site preparation and infrastructure reuse
sources.author: HGHH / Luxcara / Hamburger Energiewerke
